name: Marrakech
localTips: Download offline maps before you go — medina alleys are a maze and GPS often loses signal deep in the souks; the Koutoubia minaret is your best landmark for getting your bearings.
Dress modestly outside the riad pool — cover shoulders and knees in the medina, especially for women, to avoid unwanted attention and respect local norms.
In the souks, opening prices are theatre: counter at a third of the ask, walk away once, and settle around half — cash gets a better price than cards almost everywhere.
Unofficial 'guides' who approach you unprompted in the medina will expect payment even if you didn't ask for help — politely decline and walk toward a landmark instead.
Winter nights get properly cold despite hot days — pack a warm layer for riad courtyards and rooftop dinners even in a desert city.

activities.title: Jemaa el-Fna square
activities.description: Snake charmers and orange-juice carts by day give way to storytellers, drummers and open-air food stalls by night — Marrakech's main square never really sleeps.

activities.title: Koutoubia Mosque exterior & gardens
activities.description: The 12th-century minaret that set the height limit for the entire city — non-Muslims can't enter, but the surrounding rose gardens and floodlit facade at dusk are free.

activities.title: Medina souks wandering
activities.description: A covered labyrinth of leather, lantern, carpet and metalwork stalls stretching from Jemaa el-Fna toward the tanneries — get lost on purpose, every alley leads somewhere.

activities.title: Mellah Jewish quarter
activities.description: Marrakech's historic Jewish quarter, dating to 1558 — narrow lanes of spice merchants and jewellers near the Bahia Palace, with the Lazama Synagogue tucked mid-block.

activities.title: City ramparts walk
activities.description: 19km of pink pisé (rammed-earth) walls ring the old city, built in 1122 — walk or cycle a stretch near Bab Agnaou for the best-preserved crenellations.

activities.title: Menara Gardens
activities.description: Olive groves surround a 12th-century reflecting pool with the Atlas Mountains as backdrop — a favourite royal picnic spot since the Almohad dynasty.

activities.title: Gueliz new town streets
activities.description: Marrakech's French colonial-era district — Art Deco facades, concept boutiques and pavement cafes along Avenue Mohammed V, a different pace from the medina entirely.

activities.title: Cyber Parc Arsat Moulay Abdeslam
activities.description: A shaded 19th-century orchard-turned-public-park in the middle of the new town, with free wifi towers and quiet paths locals use to escape the medina's intensity.

activities.title: Souk Sebbaghine dyers' quarter
activities.description: Skeins of wool dripping in every colour hang overhead in the dyers' souk, coloured with saffron, mint and poppy the traditional way — most photogenic mid-morning.

activities.title: Spice souk stalls
activities.description: Pyramids of cumin, paprika, ras el hanout and dried rosebuds spill from sacks along Rahba Kedima square — browse even without buying, the colour alone is worth it.

activities.title: Bab Agnaou historic gate
activities.description: A 12th-century Almohad gate carved with Kufic script and floral reliefs — one of the finest surviving examples of Almohad monumental stonework, free to view.

activities.title: Ensemble Artisanal craft cooperative
activities.description: A state-run cooperative where you can watch weavers, leatherworkers and woodcarvers at fixed, non-haggle prices — a low-pressure way to see the crafts before souk shopping.

activities.title: Mouassine Fountain & Rahba Kedima
activities.description: A restored 16th-century public fountain that once watered the neighbourhood's animals and residents separately — now framed by boutique riads and herbalist stalls.

activities.title: Hidden rooftop cafe over Jemaa el-Fna
activities.description: Terrace cafes like Café de France or Kosybar overlook the square from above the chaos — mint tea with a view of the entire performance below, away from the touts.
activities.insiderNote: The cheaper terrace cafes on the square's north side charge a fraction of the fancier restaurant rooftops for an identical view — climb a few extra flights to compare.

activities.title: Neighbourhood public hammam
activities.description: Every medina quarter has a working hammam locals actually use daily — buckets, black soap and a kessa mitt scrub, none of the spa robes or scented candles.
activities.insiderNote: Bring your own black soap (savon noir), kessa mitt and towel, or buy a cheap kit at any medina general store first — public hammams don't provide them.

activities.title: Bab Debbagh tanneries
activities.description: Leather hides are still soaked in stone vats of pigeon-dropping and mineral dye at the edge of the medina — smaller and far less commercialised than Fez's tanneries.
activities.insiderNote: A guide's mint sprig for the smell is a nice touch but not essential — go early morning when the vats are actively being worked, not mid-afternoon when they're quiet.

activities.title: Mint tea ritual at a traditional café
activities.description: Poured from a height to aerate it, Morocco's 'Berber whisky' is a full ceremony — three glasses is traditional, the first bitter, the last sweetest as the leaves steep.
activities.insiderNote: Refusing a second glass can seem abrupt in a hosted setting — a polite 'no more, thank you, it was delicious' works better than just saying no.

activities.title: No-name medina grill stall
activities.description: Unmarked stalls off the main souk routes grill brochettes and merguez over charcoal for a fraction of restaurant prices — follow the smoke and the queue of locals.
activities.insiderNote: Point at the cuts hanging or laid out rather than trying to order by name — most of these stalls run entirely in Darija with no menu at all.

activities.title: Souk Cherifia rooftop boutiques
activities.description: A restored covered market above the main souk chaos, home to young Moroccan designers reworking traditional caftans and leather goods — fixed prices, quiet browsing.
activities.insiderNote: The rooftop terrace café above the boutiques is one of the few spots in the souk to sit down with a drink mid-shop without a hard sales pitch.

activities.title: Quiet riad courtyard lunch
activities.description: Restored guesthouses tucked behind anonymous medina doors open their tiled courtyards for lunch — orange trees, a fountain and total silence a few steps off a chaotic souk lane.
activities.insiderNote: Many riads will seat non-guests for lunch if you call ahead — it's the calmest, coolest place to eat in the medina at midday.

activities.title: Atlas Mountains day trip
activities.description: Berber villages, walnut groves and waterfalls at Ourika or Imlil an hour from the city — snow-capped peaks visible above 4,000m even in a desert-city summer.
activities.bookingQuery: Atlas Mountains day trip from Marrakech

activities.title: Sunrise hot air balloon over the desert
activities.description: Drift silently over palmeraie and pink desert scrub outside the city as the sun comes up, finishing with a traditional Berber breakfast in a tent.
activities.bookingQuery: sunrise hot air balloon ride Marrakech

activities.title: Camel ride through the Palmeraie
activities.description: A guided camel trek through the palm groves north of the city, best at sunset when the palms silhouette against the Atlas foothills.

activities.title: Sahara Desert overnight trip
activities.description: A multi-day trip over the Atlas to Merzouga's dunes — camel trek at sunset, a Berber camp under the stars, and sandboarding before the drive back.
activities.bookingQuery: Sahara desert overnight tour from Marrakech

activities.title: Moroccan cooking class
activities.description: Shop a local market with your instructor, then build a tagine from scratch — preserved lemon, olives and slow-cooked spice work explained step by step.

activities.title: Hammam & spa experience
activities.description: A full spa version of the traditional scrub — steam room, black soap exfoliation and an argan oil massage in a restored riad, robes and mint tea included.

activities.title: Quad biking in Agafay Desert
activities.description: Rocky lunar-like desert 30 minutes from the city — no sand dunes, but dramatic Atlas Mountain backdrops and far fewer tour groups than the true Sahara.

activities.title: Horse-drawn calèche tour
activities.description: A green-painted horse carriage loops the ramparts and Menara Gardens at a slow clip-clop pace — an old-fashioned way to cover ground the medina's alleys don't allow.

activities.title: Ourika Valley day trip
activities.description: A greener, closer alternative to the high Atlas — waterfalls, Berber villages and roadside walnut and argan-oil cooperatives on the way up the valley.
activities.bookingQuery: Ourika Valley day trip from Marrakech

activities.title: Henna art experience
activities.description: A henna artist hand-draws traditional Berber or Arabic geometric patterns that darken over a couple of days — done at a riad or in the square itself.

activities.title: Fantasia folklore dinner show
activities.description: A theatrical dinner of tagine and acrobats under Berber tents, followed by a fantasia — horsemen in traditional dress firing rifles in a synchronised charge.

activities.title: Guided medina walking tour
activities.description: A licensed guide navigates the souk's unmarked alleys, explaining guild history and negotiating tactics while steering you clear of commission-hungry 'helpers'.
